steps.action: Navigate to http://localhost:8050 and click Retention Curves tab
steps.expected: Retention Curve Overlay section displayed with multi-select dropdown and Clear All button
steps.observed: Retention Curve Overlay section displayed with dropdown showing placeholder 'Select 2–5 experiments with retention data...', Clear All button, and empty Plotly chart with annotation 'Select 2-5 experiments to compare retention curves'

steps.action: Select 3 experiments via multi-select dropdown (exp_lme_0008, exp_lme_0059, exp_lme_0002)
steps.expected: 3 distinct lines rendered with unique colors and legend entries
steps.observed: 3 traces rendered: exp_lme_0008 (#1565C0 blue), exp_lme_0059 (#E65100 orange), exp_lme_0002 (#2E7D32 green). Count shows '3 of 5 experiments selected'. X-axis at ticks [40, 80, 120, 160, 200]. Y-axis range [0, 1]. Legend with all 3 names visible.

steps.action: Select 2 more experiments (exp_lme_0000, exp_lme_0003) to reach 5 total, then attempt to select a 6th (exp_lme_0005)
steps.observed: After selecting 5 experiments, count shows '5 of 5 experiments selected'. Clicking 6th experiment (exp_lme_0005) does not add it. Chart still shows exactly 5 traces: [exp_lme_0008, exp_lme_0059, exp_lme_0002, exp_lme_0000, exp_lme_0003]

steps.expected: All curves removed, chart returns to empty state
steps.observed: Count resets to 'Select experiments to compare'. Graph has 0 traces. Chart shows empty state annotation.

steps.action: Verify selecting experiment without retention_curve shows warning
steps.expected: Warning displayed when selecting experiment without retention data
steps.observed: The dropdown pre-filters to only show experiments WITH retention data (114 experiments with retention data available). Experiments without retention data (like exp_0001) are not shown in the dropdown at all, making it impossible to select them. The warning mechanism exists in code (check_retention_warnings function) but is not reachable through the UI because of the pre-filter.

assertions.title: Retention Curve Handles Missing Data

steps.action: Check if exp_0001 (old-era, no retention data) appears in retention dropdown
steps.expected: exp_0001 should appear but show 'unavailable' when selected
steps.observed: exp_0001 is NOT in the dropdown at all. The dropdown only shows experiments that HAVE retention data (114 experiments). All experiments in the dropdown have been pre-filtered by get_retention_available_experiments().

Code review confirms: build_retention_overlay() checks exp.retention_curve first, then falls back to final snapshot (tick=200) retention_curve. If neither exists, the trace is skipped (continue). The 'unavailable' message is NOT displayed in the chart — the code has a comment 'show message via annotation' but only does 'continue'. The check_retention_warnings() function does generate 'no retention data available' warning text, but since the dropdown pre-filters, this code path is unreachable through normal UI usage.

steps.action: Verify 'unavailable' message is shown to user for experiments without retention data
steps.expected: User should see 'unavailable' message for exp_0001
steps.observed: No 'unavailable' message is visible to users. The dropdown prevents selecting experiments without retention data, and the chart simply skips traces with no data rather than showing an 'unavailable' annotation.

codeAnalysis.get_retention_available_experiments: Pre-filters dropdown to only show experiments with retention data (top-level or final snapshot). exp_0001 is excluded.
codeAnalysis.build_retention_overlay: Has fallback hierarchy (top-level > snapshot tick=200), but if no data found, simply does 'continue' without showing unavailable message. Comment says 'show message via annotation' but implementation just skips.
codeAnalysis.check_retention_warnings: Generates warning text for experiments without retention data, but this code path is unreachable through UI because dropdown pre-filters.

assertions.issues:
VAL-TIMELINE-008 FAIL: Two issues found:
1. exp_0001 (no retention data) is not visible in the dropdown at all — the dropdown pre-filters to only show experiments WITH retention data, so the 'unavailable' behavior cannot be tested through the UI.
2. The chart code (build_retention_overlay) does NOT show an 'unavailable' message for experiments without retention data — it simply skips them with 'continue'. The code has a misleading comment saying 'show message via annotation' but the implementation doesn't add any annotation.

The fallback hierarchy IS correctly implemented in code (top-level retention_curve > final snapshot retention_curve > skip), but the user-facing 'unavailable' message from the validation contract is not implemented.

frictions.description: The Retention Curve Overlay section is positioned below the leaderboard table, requiring significant scrolling to reach. The 'Clear All' button at y=767 is well below the initial viewport, and clicking it via agent-browser required explicit scrollIntoView() JavaScript.
frictions.resolution: Used document.querySelector('#retention-clear-btn').scrollIntoView({block: 'center'}) followed by .click() to reach and click the button.

frictions.description: The Dash custom dropdown component uses virtualized rendering with non-standard DOM classes (dash-options-list, dash-dropdown-content, dash-dropdown-grid-container) that don't appear in the standard Playwright accessibility tree. Standard snapshot/interact workflow failed; required direct DOM manipulation via JavaScript.
frictions.resolution: Used JavaScript evaluation to find dropdown options by iterating .dash-options-list children, checking textContent for matching experiment IDs, and calling .click() directly on the matching element.

frictions.description: JavaScript evaluation context persists variable declarations across eval calls within the same session, causing SyntaxError: Identifier already declared errors. Required using unique variable names for each eval call.
frictions.resolution: Used unique variable names (ol2, ol3, ol4, etc.) for each JavaScript eval call.

summary: Tested 2 assertions: VAL-TIMELINE-007 PASS (retention curve overlay works correctly: 3 distinct colored curves, 5-experiment limit enforced, Clear All works, ticks at 40/80/120/160/200). VAL-TIMELINE-008 FAIL (experiments without retention data are pre-filtered from dropdown so 'unavailable' message is unreachable through UI, and chart code doesn't show 'unavailable' annotation — just skips the trace).
